schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The first real device on the spine — the system's notary and its proving mechanism (MAP.md:569). `TesterDevice` subclasses `BaseDevice` (so it carries CP1-CP6 and reports the Form v0 #2 surface), and its one capability today is `run_proof(path)`: run a proof file as a subprocess and PRODUCE a VALIDATION record — the ratified eight fields (claim, caller, date, method, verdict, evidence, falsifier, horizon). The verdict is read from the subject's exit code, not granted by the tester.
why: Law 8 — nothing enters proven-space without a proof a hollow build couldn't pass; the verdict must come from a hand the builder did not guide. Placing that gate in a device (not a convention each builder self-applies) is Law 4 (physics over policy): the proving and the attesting come from the same hand, so a green cannot be self-granted. This is the smallest honest version of the tester — it proves, it attests, it dogfoods (its first act validates the real base proofs) — while the pillars it will grow (network ownership, a durable store, richer verdict methods) stay filed edges, built when a real need pulls them (round-three discipline: simplest provable stone first).
invoke: import: `from cairn.tester.device import TesterDevice`. Prove: `python3 cairn/tester/proofs/test_tester.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: device — the first runtime component on the spine (base/ is the code-seam it stands on).
entry: FRESH — authored to Cairn's Form v0, not grafted (like device.py/shim.py). UU's tester (`UnseenUniversity/unseen_university/devices/tester/` — ~1226 lines: netpolicy 381, isolation 183, sandbox, seal) is the network-sandbox pillar Cairn will reach as a LATER stone. Only MAP's ratified VALIDATION schema (the eight fields, MAP.md:569) crosses, and it crosses as a record shape, not as carried code. UU's own verdict shape (seal_confirmed/seal_detail/duration_s) did not cross — it is the network-seal model, not the Cairn VALIDATION.
state: PROVED for the minimal stone: TesterDevice runs a proof as a subprocess and produces a well-formed VALIDATION; a KNOWN-GREEN fixture validates green and a KNOWN-RED fixture validates red (the hollow-tester killer — verified: an always-green tester trips test_red_proof_validates_red; a nine-field record trips the schema tooth). It is the FIRST real subject the base's armed composition trap actually bites on (test_composition read '0 subclasses' until now). Proven green x2 cwds, no install. OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) VALIDATIONS are PRODUCED, not yet PERSISTED — the durable greppable store is db_domain's stone (CLAUDE.md pending rule: durable state goes through the store primitives); this device returns records and writes nothing, so class-space stays state-free; (b) the kernel-owned network + build isolation/sandbox (UU's netpolicy/isolation) are NOT built — this tester runs local proofs, it does not yet guard a build's network (the next stone on this pillar, MAP.md:888); (c) the verdict METHOD is exit-code only — quorum / review-by-N-experts for concept pieces (MAP.md:752) is a later method the `method` field already names room for; (d) reported-vs-filed intention drift detection is not wired — `intention()` returns a live dict; comparing it to this charter is a later capability.
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_tester.py, runnable bare (python3) today; the tester will eventually own how proofs run, including its own. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) `run_proof` on a passing proof yields anything but green, or on a failing proof anything but red (the notary must not lie); (2) a VALIDATION record's field-set is not exactly the ratified eight (drift — extra or missing field); (3) `TesterDevice` stops subclassing `BaseDevice` or drops CP1-CP6 (trips the base's composition trap); (4) `introspect()` stops reporting intention -> state -> settings in order.
how_it_learns: Yes — and it is where learning-from-proof concentrates. Every VALIDATION carries falsifier + horizon (Law 3), so each verdict knows how it expires — 'validated on the 17th' can itself go stale and be re-run; a verdict is a best-guess-with-an-expiry, not a permanent stamp. A red is not a failure to suppress but CP2 data (FAIL = Further Advance In Learning), kicked back to design. The component's own horizon: its verdict METHOD is provisional — exit-code today, quorum / review-by-experts for concept pieces later (MAP.md:752); which method earns its keep is learned from what it lets through vs what it catches. Once db_domain gives VALIDATIONS a durable home, that store is the evidence trail that turns answered proofs into structure (Law 1) instead of re-derivation — a mind greps it on a hunch rather than re-proving.
traces_to: Law 8 (a proof a hollow build couldn't pass — the tester IS that gate)
Law 4 (physics over policy — proving + attesting from one hand)
Law 3 (a VALIDATION expires: falsifier + horizon ride along)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address)
Form v0 #2 (the ordered introspection surface, MAP.md:157)
owner: CC owns the tester's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the VALIDATION schema's ratification (MAP.md:569) and the node-class definitions.
proof: proofs/test_tester.py — device-hood + the six, Form v0 #2 order, green->green, red->red (the hollow-tester killer), state reflects runs, and it exercises the base's armed trap on its first real device; plus a __main__ dogfood that validates the real base proofs. proofs/fixtures/ — a green (exit 0) and a red (exit 1) proof for the tester to point at. Green: `python3 cairn/tester/proofs/test_tester.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-17 as the third step of the code spine (base -> base pair -> tester), the first real DEVICE. Opens the 'tester + kernel-owned network' spine step (MAP.md:888); the network, the durable VALIDATIONS store, and richer verdict methods are the next stones on this pillar, deferred until a real need pulls them.
